name: Italy (1946–2003)

colors.symbolism: the Italian national colours; traditionally a bright grass-green shade

description: A green, white and red vertical tricolour of three equal bands, in the brighter traditional colour shades used before the 2003 Pantone specification.
history: The plain tricolour adopted by the Italian Republic when the monarchy was abolished, used from 1946 until 2003 when the government first specified the colours formally via a Pantone circular. This earlier version used a comparatively bright, grass-green and lighter red, distinguishing it from the deeper 2003 shades and the final 2006 standard.
